body:
## Feature Request

Currently, Google Docs URLs are always captured via the `/export?format=html` endpoint, regardless of the `--capture` flag value. Even `--capture browser` auto-detects Google Docs URLs and routes them through the export API. This export produces HTML that doesn't always match the live Google Docs editor rendering (row order, cell layout, and content differences in complex table-heavy documents).

## Problem

The `/export?format=html` endpoint is the least faithful representation of a Google Doc. Complex documents with tables containing multiple images per cell, merged cells, or specific formatting can render differently compared to what the user sees in the editor.

Additionally, suggested edit content (images added via suggestions) is **completely stripped** from the export — they don't appear in the exported HTML at all.

## Google Docs URL routes and their data freshness

| Route | Data | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `/edit` | **Fresh, complete** | Contains `DOCS_modelChunk` in `<script>` tags — the full document model including suggested edits. Also contains CID-to-`docs-images-rt` URL mapping. Canvas-based rendering (no DOM content). |
| `/preview` | **May be stale** | For some documents, `/preview` serves outdated content that doesn't reflect recent edits. Also uses canvas rendering. |
| `/pub` | **Requires "Publish to web"** | Only works if the document owner has explicitly published it. Redirects to login otherwise. |
| `/mobilebasic` | **May be stale** | Serves HTML DOM (not canvas), but can show outdated data for some documents. Doesn't include suggested edits. |
| `/export?format=html` | **May be stale, lossy** | Can serve outdated content for some documents. Strips suggested edits, may restructure table rows differently than the editor shows, and re-encodes images as base64. |

**Only the `/edit` route is guaranteed to have fresh, complete data** — including suggested edits and the full document model. The document model (`DOCS_modelChunk`) is embedded in `<script>` tags and contains the same data used to render the canvas.

## Proposed solution

Support three distinct capture methods for Google Docs, selectable by the user:

### 1. Public Export API (current behavior)
- Uses `https://docs.google.com/document/d/<DOC_ID>/export?format=html`
- No authentication required for public documents
- Fastest, but least faithful to editor rendering
- May serve stale content for some documents
- Strips suggested edit content entirely
- Should be the default for `--capture api` when no `--api-token` is provided

### 2. Google Docs REST API (`docs.googleapis.com`)
- Uses `https://docs.googleapis.com/v1/documents/<DOC_ID>` 
- Returns structured JSON document tree
- Requires OAuth2 authentication (could use `--api-token` or a service account)
- More faithful representation of document structure
- Should be used when `--capture api` is combined with `--api-token`

### 3. Browser DOM capture via `DOCS_modelChunk`
- Navigates to the `/edit` page in a headless browser
- Intercepts `DOCS_modelChunk` from `<script>` tags — the JSON document model
- Extracts CID-to-URL mapping from page scripts (`docs-images-rt` URLs)
- Most faithful to what the user sees in the editor
- **Includes suggested edits** that other methods drop
- `docs-images-rt` image URLs are publicly accessible (no auth needed to download)
- Should be used when `--capture browser` is explicitly specified — currently this flag is ignored for Google Docs URLs

See the [comment below](https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/issues/72#issuecomment-4259395295) for a working JavaScript example using [browser-commander](https://github.com/link-foundation/browser-commander).

## Expected behavior

| Flag | No token | With token |
|------|----------|------------|
| `--capture api` | Public export (`/export?format=html`) | Google Docs REST API (`docs.googleapis.com`) |
| `--capture browser` | Browser DOM capture (headless, via `DOCS_modelChunk`) | Browser DOM capture (headless, with auth cookie/session) |

The key change: `--capture browser` should **not** auto-redirect to the export API for Google Docs. It should honor the user's explicit choice and use the browser engine to extract the document model.

## Applies to

- [ ] Rust CLI
- [ ] JavaScript CLI

Both implementations currently auto-detect Google Docs URLs and bypass browser capture.
title: Google Docs: support all capture methods (public export, Docs API, browser DOM)
